$description: Motion token baseline set — Ch. 25 reference implementation. Levels 1–3 of the maturity scale. Spring configs use a custom type pending DTCG spec extension.

duration.$description: Primitive duration scale. Values are raw design decisions — brand-variable.

easing.$description: Primitive easing curves. Each curve is a single concept — the 4 control points are not individually tokenizable.

spring.$description: Primitive spring configs. Custom type — DTCG does not yet define a spring type. Resolver needed to transform to CSS linear() for web targets. See spring-resolver.js.

crisp.$description: Tight, precise — critically damped (no overshoot). For controls, toggles, precise spatial changes.

gentle.$description: Soft, unhurried settle. For panels, drawers, medium-weight transitions.

bouncy.$description: Visible overshoot — expressive brand motion only. Not safe for opacity or color. Long settlement (~885ms) is intentional — use only for deliberate brand moments.

$extensions.warning: dampingRatio 0.35 — significant overshoot. Spatial properties only (position, scale). Never on opacity or color. 885ms settlement: suitable for brand moments, not micro-interactions.

motion.$description: Semantic layer — named aliases that reference primitives and carry intent.

duration.$description: Named duration intents. Reference primitives; carry usage meaning.

instant.$description: Imperceptible state feedback — hover states, active states

default.$description: Standard UI transitions — tab switch, dropdown open

deliberate.$description: Intentional storytelling — onboarding, empty states, celebrations

easing.$description: Named easing intents. Reference primitives; define the role of the curve.

enter.$description: Element arriving on screen or gaining prominence. Decelerates to communicate weight.

exit.$description: Element leaving screen or losing prominence. Accelerates to communicate release. Use only for final exits — not for elements that will return.

move.$description: Repositioning within screen — element changes position but stays visible.

emphasize.$description: Drawing attention — signature, expressive movement. Use sparingly.

spring.$description: Named spring intent aliases. Reference spring configs; define the character.

crisp.$description: Sharp response — controls, precise interactions, spatial feedback without overshoot.

gentle.$description: Soft settle — panels, drawers, medium-weight transitions.

bouncy.$description: Expressive overshoot — brand moments, playful interactions. Spatial only.

transition.$description: Semantic composite transitions (depth 3). Combine duration + easing into named patterns. DTCG transition type.

enter.$description: Standard element entrance — modal, panel, overlay appears.

exit.$description: Standard element exit. Fast + accelerating communicates decisiveness.

expand.$description: Expansion — accordion, collapsible content, growing container.

feedback.$description: Immediate response to user action — button press, checkbox check. Should feel instantaneous.

stagger.$description: Application-level relationship tokens (depth 4). Describe timing relationships between multiple elements, not properties of a single element. Require context (element count, layout) to resolve.

$extensions.note: These tokens cannot resolve to a CSS property alone — they require a JS resolver that knows the element count. See backlog for implementation.

list.$description: Vertical list entrance — each item enters with a 20ms offset, capped at 500ms total.
